senses.definition: a lengthy article in a newspaper or magazine, often written by a specialist about a certain topic

examples.x: The feature explored the impact of technology on education.
The magazine's latest issue includes a feature on sustainable living.
She wrote a feature about the rise of electric cars for the newspaper.
The travel magazine published a feature on hidden gems in Europe.
His feature on urban gardening received a lot of positive feedback.

senses.definition: a section of a newspaper or magazine that regularly publishes articles about a particular subject

examples.x: The sports column provides updates and commentary on local teams.
Her column focuses on tips for healthy living.
The magazine’s fashion column reviews the latest trends.
He writes a column about technology every month.
Each week, the newspaper features a political column by a well-known journalist.

senses.definition: a section or part in a newspaper in which people are given advice regarding their personal problems

examples.x: I always enjoy reading the advice column in my favorite magazine for tips on managing stress.
The advice column often tackles difficult topics like finances and relationships.
After a tough breakup, I turned to the advice column for some guidance on how to move on.
I always look forward to the advice column because it offers practical solutions to everyday problems.
I found some helpful suggestions in the advice column about dealing with family conflicts.

senses.definition: a segment in a newspaper assigned to stories about the lives of the celebrities

examples.x: She picked up the latest issue of the newspaper to read the gossip column.
The gossip column was full of juicy rumors about the celebrity's new relationship.
Everyone in the office was talking about the latest gossip column that mentioned their favorite actor.
The gossip column reported that the singer had been spotted at a fancy restaurant with a mystery date.
Some gossip columns focus only on fashion and lifestyle.

senses.definition: a segment in a newspaper or magazine devoted to personal messages or advertisements

examples.x: The personal column of the newspaper is filled with birthday messages and congratulations.
They found a small ad in the personal column offering a used car for sale.
He checked the personal column every day to see if there were any job offers.
In the personal column, they posted a reminder about the community fundraiser next week.

senses.definition: a newspaper report, usually sent from another town or a foreign country, often on a military-related matter

examples.x: The war correspondent filed a detailed dispatch from the front lines.
Readers eagerly awaited the latest dispatches from the conflict zone.
The editor-in-chief reviewed the dispatches before publication to ensure accuracy and relevance.
His dispatch provided crucial insights into the humanitarian crisis unfolding overseas.
The newspaper published an urgent dispatch about the diplomatic negotiations in Europe.

senses.definition: a newspaper article expressing the views of the editor on a particular subject

examples.x: The editorial argued for more investment in public transportation.
She wrote an editorial about the importance of voting in local elections.
His editorial criticized the government's handling of the economic crisis.
The latest editorial highlighted the need for healthcare reform.

senses.definition: a forecast of an individual's future based on their zodiac sign or date of birth, especially as published in a newspaper or magazine
a piece published in a newspaper or magazine that describes a movie, or a TV show before it is aired
a type of periodical publication that features critical essays or evaluations of contemporary literature, art, or current events

examples.x: He submitted his article to an academic review.
The art review featured an in-depth critique of the exhibition.
She regularly writes for a cultural review.
The journal is a well-known political review.

senses.definition: an article published in a newspaper or magazine that praises a movie, book, etc.

examples.x: The new Broadway show received rave reviews from critics.
Her latest novel earned rave reviews for its compelling storytelling.
The film got rave reviews and quickly became a box office hit.
After its premiere, the documentary earned rave reviews from audiences.
His performance in the play was met with rave reviews.

senses.definition: a brief piece in a newspaper printed next to the main article, providing additional information

examples.x: The sidebar accompanying the main article provided additional context and background information on the topic.
The newspaper's website features interactive sidebars with multimedia elements, such as videos and infographics.
A sidebar column offers readers a different perspective on current events, written by a guest columnist.
The sidebar highlighted key statistics and data relevant to the main story, providing readers with a quick overview.
Readers can find links to related articles and resources in the sidebar of the online edition, enhancing their understanding of the topic.

senses.definition: a time, usually in summer, when newspapers are filled with insignificant stories because there are not that many important news

examples.x: During the silly season, the news was filled with stories about unusual animal sightings and quirky events.
The newspapers were full of gossip and celebrity updates; it was clearly the silly season.
With no major news breaking, the silly season seemed to drag on with trivial stories.
It was the silly season, so we had to settle for reading about a man who set a world record for wearing the most hats.

senses.definition: newspapers, journalists, and magazines as a whole

examples.x: He gave an interview to the press about his new book.
The press covered the event extensively, with multiple stories published.
The press often influences public opinion with its coverage of political issues.
After the announcement, the press was quick to report on the reactions.
Public figures are frequently in the spotlight of the press.

senses.definition: the most recent and important news that is added to a newspaper at the last moment before printing or after the start of the printing process, especially as a heading

examples.x: The breaking news was so significant that the editor ordered a stop press to update the front page of the newspaper.
As soon as the journalist received the urgent update, they rushed to the editor's desk to request a stop press.
The stop press announcement informed readers about a last-minute change to the schedule of the upcoming event.
The newspaper's website posted a stop press alert, directing readers to the latest updates on the developing story.
The breaking news was so urgent that the editor shouted "Stop press!"

senses.definition: a newspaper that is published on a large piece of paper regarded as more serious

examples.x: The broadsheet featured a detailed analysis of the economic implications of the new policy.
Broadsheets are known for their extensive reporting on international news and politics.
The editorials in the broadsheet encouraged readers to engage in civic discourse.
The broadsheet's arts section reviewed the latest exhibitions and cultural events in the city.
The journalist wrote an investigative piece that was published on the front page of the broadsheet.

senses.definition: a humorous drawing on the topics that are covered in the news, usually published in a newspaper or magazine

examples.x: Each issue features a cartoon that comments on current events.
The newspaper's cartoon poked fun at the recent political debate.
The editorial cartoon made light of the latest sports controversy.
She collects cartoons that reflect political and social issues.
Cartoons often use satire to comment on social and political issues.

senses.definition: the middle pages of a magazine or newspaper that face each other

examples.x: The magazine’s center spread featured a stunning photo of the city’s skyline at sunset.
The restaurant’s ad took up the entire center spread of the food magazine this month.
She flipped through the magazine until she reached the center spread with the fashion layout.
The center spread of the travel guide gave an overview of the top destinations to visit this year.

senses.definition: the number of copies of a newspaper or magazine sold at regular intervals

examples.x: The newspaper's circulation figures reached an all-time high after the launch of its digital subscription service.
Despite the decline in print media, the magazine saw an increase in circulation thanks to its engaging content and multimedia platforms.
The publisher announced a decrease in circulation numbers, prompting a reassessment of distribution strategies.
Advertisers are particularly interested in publications with high circulation numbers as they offer greater exposure to potential customers.
The editor attributed the success of the magazine to its loyal readership, which has contributed to steady circulation figures over the years.

senses.definition: a story or article cut from a newspaper or magazine to be kept

examples.x: My grandmother keeps a box of cherished clippings that she has collected over the years, filled with articles of historical significance.
The journalist's office was cluttered with stacks of clippings, each representing a story they had written or found noteworthy.
She carefully added the latest clipping to her scrapbook, documenting memorable moments and achievements.
As a journalist, I often save clippings of my published articles as a record of my work and accomplishments.

senses.definition: a unit of measurement used in print media to determine the amount of space occupied by an article, advertisement, or other content within a column of a newspaper or magazine

examples.x: The article took up two column inches on the front page.
The editor asked the writer to shorten the piece to fit within three column inches.
The reporter's story was given four column inches, which was typical for a feature article.
The local business purchased ten column inches of ad space for the upcoming issue.

senses.definition: a type of newspaper that is published everyday except Sunday

examples.x: He reads the local daily every morning with his coffee.
The political scandal was the top story in all the major dailies.
She works as a journalist for a respected national daily.
The trial's progress was documented in the city's leading daily.
The crossword puzzle in the daily is his favorite part of the newspaper.

senses.definition: a publication, such as a newspaper or magazine, that is released once a week

examples.x: He enjoys reading the local weekly to stay informed about community events.
The weekly includes in-depth articles and interviews with notable figures.
They subscribe to a fashion weekly to keep up with the latest trends.
She writes a column for a popular weekly, sharing her thoughts on lifestyle topics.

senses.definition: something that occurs or is published every two weeks, typically referring to a publication, such as a newspaper or magazine, that is issued or updated once every two weeks
the total number of copies of a newspaper, book, etc. that is published at one time
a type of newspaper that is published after midday

examples.x: I like reading the evening paper to catch up on the day's events after work.
He picked up the evening paper on his way home to see the latest sports scores.
The evening paper reported on the surprise announcement made earlier in the day.
I missed the morning news, but the evening paper had all the updates.

senses.definition: the first and main page of a newspaper in which important news pieces are printed

examples.x: The shocking news made it to the front page of the newspaper.
The front page featured a photo of the president’s visit to the city.
His story was so important that it was printed on the front page.
The front page of the magazine showcased the latest fashion trends.
She was excited to see her article on the front page.

senses.definition: an official journal or newspaper that contains serious information about decision making and policies, published by an organization

examples.x: The local gazette publishes articles on community events, local news, and editorials.
I enjoy reading the legal gazette to stay informed about recent court decisions and legislative changes.
The official gazette of the government contains announcements, regulations, and official notices.
The historical society maintains archives of old gazettes, providing valuable insights into the past.
The university gazette features research highlights, faculty profiles, and campus news for the academic community.

senses.definition: the large words in the upper part of a page of a newspaper, article, etc.

examples.x: The headline of today’s newspaper reads, "Major Earthquake Strikes the City."
The headline caught everyone's attention with its bold statement about the politician’s sudden resignation.
The headline on the front page was so shocking that it immediately became the topic of conversation in the office.
The headline highlighted the economic crisis, urging readers to turn to the inside pages for detailed coverage.
As soon as the headline was published, social media exploded with reactions from readers around the world.

senses.definition: the title of a magazine or newspaper at the top of the first page

examples.x: The masthead of the newspaper displayed the publication's name in bold letters at the top of the front page.
The magazine's masthead listed the editor-in-chief, managing editor, and contributing writers.
The design team updated the masthead to reflect the publication's new logo and branding.
The masthead also included the publication's contact information, website URL, and social media handles.
The masthead of the newsletter featured a striking graphic design that captured the attention of readers.

senses.definition: an article or report, especially in a newspaper, published soon after the death of a person, typically containing details about their life

examples.x: Writing an obituary can be a way for loved ones to celebrate and remember the life of the deceased.
The family requested that donations be made to a charitable organization in lieu of flowers, as noted in the obituary.
The obituary included details about the funeral arrangements and memorial service for those wishing to pay their respects.
Friends and family members shared fond memories and anecdotes in the guestbook accompanying the online obituary.

senses.definition: an article that has been separately published as a piece in a magazine or newspaper

examples.x: The author received offprints of the article from the publisher to distribute to colleagues and peers.
The library keeps offprints of rare and valuable manuscripts in its special collections for scholarly research.
Researchers often request offprints of articles from academic journals to cite in their own work.
The professor shared offprints of his latest research findings with students interested in the topic.
The conference organizers provided offprints of the keynote speaker's presentation to attendees as a souvenir.

senses.definition: a section in the newspaper that contains personal opinions about the news and feature articles, opposite the editorial page

examples.x: The newspaper's op-ed section features contributions from a diverse range of voices, offering readers varying perspectives on current events.
The columnist wrote an op-ed criticizing the government's handling of the crisis, sparking debate and discussion among readers.
Submitting an op-ed to a newspaper is a way for individuals to share their opinions and insights on important issues with a broader audience.
The op-ed argued for the need to address climate change through bold policy initiatives and collective action.
The editor invited the renowned scholar to write an op-ed on the implications of artificial intelligence for society, which generated considerable interest among readers.

senses.definition: a newspaper, periodical, or magazine published by a particular group or organization to promote their views

examples.x: The party's official organ serves as a platform for disseminating its political agenda and promoting party policies.
The trade union publishes an organ to inform members about labor issues, negotiations, and upcoming events.
The religious organization's organ features articles on spiritual teachings, community events, and charitable activities.
The environmental group's organ advocates for conservation efforts, environmental policies, and sustainable practices.
The student organization's organ provides a forum for students to express their opinions, share experiences, and discuss campus issues.

senses.definition: the written content or text that is prepared for publication, such as articles, headlines, advertisements, or any textual material that appears in newspapers, magazines, brochures, or other printed materials
a part of a magazine, newspaper, etc. that can be taken out easily and kept separately

examples.x: She always saves the recipe pull-out from the magazine to try new dishes at home.
The travel pull-out in today’s paper features top destinations for budget vacations.
I found the job listings I needed in the employment pull-out section.
The kids were excited to see the comic strip pull-out included in the magazine this month.
The newspaper’s education pull-out provides insights into scholarships and exam tips.

senses.definition: a newspaper or magazine that mostly contains stories about the bad behavior and private lives of the celebrities

examples.x: Many people avoid reading scandal sheets because they focus too much on rumors.
The newsstand was filled with colorful scandal sheets, each one promising juicy details.
The scandal sheet reported exaggerated details of the politician's private affairs.
She picked up a scandal sheet to see the latest gossip about the royal family.
He found a scandal sheet at the grocery store and was shocked by the outrageous claims.

senses.definition: a separate section, usually in the form of a colored magazine, sold with a newspaper
a newspaper with smaller pages and many pictures, covering stories about famous people and not much serious news

examples.x: The tabloid sensationalized the celebrity scandal with exaggerated headlines and paparazzi photos.
Despite its reputation for gossip, some tabloids also cover serious news stories alongside celebrity gossip.
The supermarket checkout line is lined with tabloids featuring the latest celebrity gossip and scandalous headlines.
The tabloid's front page featured a shocking exposé about a politician's personal life.
Tabloids often rely on anonymous sources and speculative reporting to attract readers with sensational stories.

senses.definition: a printed work, such as a book, magazine, etc. that is publicly distributed
a publication focused on classified advertisements, enabling individuals and businesses to promote products, services, or job opportunities in categorized sections

examples.x: I found a job opening in the classified magazine that looked perfect for me.
She bought a classified magazine to look for a second-hand car.
He placed an ad in the classified magazine to sell his old bicycle.
I like browsing through the classified magazine to see if there are any good deals.
They were able to find a local moving company by looking in the classified magazine.

senses.definition: a print publication that delivers news and information specific to a particular community or region, serving as a primary source of local news for residents

examples.x: I picked up the local newspaper this morning to read about the community festival.
She subscribed to the local newspaper to stay updated on local events.
He saw his business advertised in the local newspaper last week.
The local newspaper is covering a big story about the city’s new recycling program.
My grandmother always reads the local newspaper to keep up with what’s happening in town.

senses.definition: an affordable, uncoated paper with a rough texture, commonly used for printing newspapers due to its lightweight and absorbent properties

examples.x: The local newspaper printed on newsprint, giving it a characteristic rough feel.
The advertisement was printed on cheap newsprint to keep costs low.
The ink smudged easily because the newspaper used cheap newsprint for the article.
They recycled old newsprint to create packaging material for shipping products.

senses.definition: a line that gives the writer's name, usually at the beginning or end of a column

examples.x: The journalist was proud to see her byline on the front page of the newspaper, crediting her for the investigative report.
As a freelancer, he often pitches story ideas to editors in hopes of securing a byline in prestigious publications.
The byline identifies the author of the article, giving readers insight into the person behind the words.
The editor decided to remove the byline from the article to maintain the publication's editorial voice.
Getting a byline in a reputable magazine can help writers build their portfolio and credibility in the industry.

senses.definition: a magazine or newspaper that gives information about a specific topic

examples.x: He subscribed to a travel journal to get tips for his upcoming vacation.
Many students find academic journals useful for their research projects.
The business journal reported on the recent changes in market trends.
She found a fascinating article in a health journal about new fitness trends.

senses.definition: a newspaper or magazine that enables individuals to regularly receive and access the publication's content
the distribution of articles, columns, comics, or other content to multiple newspapers or publications for publication, allowing wider readership and exposure to the content across various outlets
an enthusiastic article published in a magazine or newspaper about a particular film, book, etc.

examples.x: The magazine published a rave about the groundbreaking research being conducted in renewable energy.
Her blog post received raves from readers who found her personal story of overcoming adversity deeply inspiring.
The newspaper's feature article on the local charity received raves for its heartfelt portrayal of the organization's impact on the community.
The online review of the new art exhibition was a rave, praising the artists' creativity and talent.
The travel magazine's rave about the hidden gems of the Mediterranean coast inspired many readers to plan their next vacation.

senses.definition: a written account in a newspaper to review a book, performance, or event

examples.x: The employee received a glowing write-up from their supervisor for their exceptional performance on the project.
The restaurant received a favorable write-up in the local newspaper, praising its innovative cuisine and welcoming atmosphere.
The teacher provided detailed write-ups of each student's progress and areas for improvement in their quarterly reports.
The software company received a negative write-up in a tech blog, highlighting flaws in their latest product release.
The travel magazine published a feature write-up on the picturesque coastal town, enticing readers to visit its scenic attractions.

senses.definition: an earlier copy of a magazine or a newspaper

examples.x: The library has a vast collection of back issues of popular magazines for patrons to borrow.
The bookstore sells back issues of vintage comics, allowing collectors to complete their collections.
I subscribed to the online archive service to access back issues of academic journals for my research.
The newspaper website offers digital access to back issues dating back several decades.
The magazine editor decided to reprint a back issue featuring a popular article due to high demand from readers.
